chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q1_analysis: The paper describes the implementation using a Golang library but does not provide any public links to the source code, datasets, or artifacts.
chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q2_analysis: The evaluation is conducted on commodity servers (physical testbeds) with specific hardware configurations and network parameters (40 servers, 16-core CPU, 200 Mbps bandwidth), which qualifies as a physical testbed deployment.
chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q3_analysis: A clear, explicit section (Section III) defines the system model, stating it considers $n$ replicas where $f$ may fail arbitrarily (Byzantine failures) and requires $n \ge 3f+1$. This constitutes a formal fault model definition.
chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q4_analysis: The paper provides rigorous, data-driven analysis of performance trade-offs by comparing throughput and latency between Marlin and HotStuff under various failure scenarios ($f=1$ to $f=30$) and different request sizes (no-op vs. 150 bytes), quantifying the percentage improvements/degradations.

major_weakness: The paper lacks explicit links to public repositories for the implemented code and evaluation artifacts.
